articles.title: ‘TOUGH PILL TO SWALLOW': LADBIBLE BOSS ON THE TRAFFIC HIT FROM META'S FEED SHAKE-UP
articles.summary: Digital publishers are getting squeezed from both sides. Meta is prioritizing creator content over publisher links, cutting ad revenue, while Google's AI Overviews reduce search traffic by answering queries directly. Some publishers report CTR drops as high as 89%. Search traffic is projected to fall over 40% in three years, after steep declines from Facebook and X. A new UK ruling allows publishers to block Overviews without losing search rankings, adding leverage.

articles.title: AI WON'T SAVE ADVERTISING
articles.summary: AI hype is sweeping advertising, expectations are overblown. Fully automated ad creation isn't realistic and risks hurting the ecosystem. Like programmatic before it, AI won't replace human judgment. Its real value is improving productivity and workflows. Meanwhile, creators are becoming full-stack businesses, blurring lines with agencies. The industry is shifting toward data-driven marketing, but AI alone won't deliver the transformation many expect.

articles.title: ADD YOUR BEST CASE STUDIES IN YOUR FOOTER
articles.summary: Placing key customer proof in your website footer can influence how AI systems describe your product. In a test, Clay surfaced its case studies in the footer, and ChatGPT pulled listed customers directly from those links when asked which brands use the product. Companies that bury case studies deeper on their sites were cited far less.

articles.title: 4 WAYS TO NOT LET YOUR LOYALTY PROGRAM LIVE IN A SILO
articles.summary: Most loyalty programs fail because they're barely visible. Email flows should surface points, rewards, and status in welcome, post-purchase, and ongoing campaigns. Subscription flows should reinforce value with milestone updates and highlight what's lost during cancellation. Landing pages should surface perks upfront instead of burying them in footers. Retail experiences should connect online and in-store so that earning and redemption work both ways.

articles.title: A GOOGLE ADS TARGETING TACTIC THAT CUT INVALID CLICKS BY 50%
articles.summary: Use Google Ads audience targeting as a filter to reduce invalid clicks when click fraud is hurting your campaign performance. One account cut its invalid click rate by 50% after adding hundreds of Google predefined audiences and setting them to Targeting instead of Observation. The approach limits ads to users who match both keywords and Google's audience signals. It works best for accounts with unusually high invalid click rates because it can also exclude legitimate users who do not fit Google's predefined audiences.

articles.title: REACH 7 MILLION ENGAGED TECH PROFESSIONALS
articles.summary: Ads get ignored on social media but not in TLDR! Reach developers, PMs, marketers, founders and other tech leaders where they actually

articles.title: pay attention. Learn more about sponsorship opportunities.
CONTENTGRAPHER
articles.summary: ContentGrapher provides website content recommendations for AI visibility. You give it a URL, and it maps weak or missing concepts, how well you've connected certain concepts, and if there's anything that belongs on its own page. It's free for the first 5 analyses, then starts at $29.

articles.title: WHAT SUCCESSFUL PEOPLE TOLERATE (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: Success is framed as a function of what tradeoffs can be sustained, not just strengths or effort. Kristen Lowe introduces “success currencies”, highlighting costs like risk, rejection, and uncertainty as key drivers of achievement. The session includes a self-assessment to identify which costs drain or motivate, and whether current ambitions align with the prices required to reach them. It will take place on July 8 at 9 AM PT.

articles.title: MY HONEST OPINION ABOUT PERSONAL BRANDING IN B2B
GOOGLE JUST PUBLISHED ITS PLAYBOOK FOR KILLING AI SLOP AT SCALE
articles.summary: Google's new system targets coordinated AI content, not individual pages. It detects clusters of accounts producing templated content at scale using shared infrastructure, velocity, and semantic patterns, then removes the entire network. This shifts enforcement from optimizing single pages to avoiding detectable production patterns. High-volume, templated strategies are now a liability.

articles.title: HOW TO FIND OUT WHY A METRIC DROPPED
articles.summary: A 6-step framework to investigate and explain a metric drop.

articles.summary: MrBeast leads the ranking, followed by Dhar Mann and Steven Bartlett.

articles.title: AVOID ALL-CAPS HEADLINES, SAYS CRO PRO
articles.summary: One test replacing all-caps headlines with standard capitalization increased conversions by 25%, highlighting the impact of readability.

articles.title: SOCCER'S ASCENSION IN AMERICA
articles.summary: Soccer wins on community, accessibility, and global identity over traditional violence-heavy sports.
